mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year out-of-sample walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength across durable and non-durable sectors, which drives higher corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict direction when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive relationship between INDPRO growth and SPX returns reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during periods of rising interest rates, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS levels show zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 10-year out-of-sample evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized expansion across macroeconomic output and consumer spending channels, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate model over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals structural labor market weakness and reduced aggregate demand, which suppresses corporate profit growth and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative predictive relationship between UNRATE and SPX returns disappears or reverses during recessionary periods, or the signal fails to achieve a Rank IC > 0.03 in out-of-sample recession windows.
